text: In software development, testing plays a crucial role in ensuring code quality. Unit tests check individual functions or components to verify they work correctly in isolation. Integration tests, on the other hand, examine how different parts of the system interact together, catching issues that unit tests may miss. Many developers use test-driven development (TDD), where they write tests before writing the actual code. This approach helps them think through requirements and design more carefully. Overall, thorough testing builds confidence in the software. When developers know their code passes reliable tests, they can make changes and add new features with less fear of breaking existing functionality.
